---
name: 2. Themes in / themes out
about: Define what belongs in the work and what should stay out
title: "Themes: "
labels: ["first action", "themes"]
assignees: ""
---

## Themes In

What should this project actively explore?


## Themes Out

What should it avoid, reject, or refuse to centre?


## Tone To Protect

Examples: funny, sexy, officecore, liminal, weirdly competent, antiwar, romantic, technical, tender, absurd, eerie.


## Tone To Avoid

Examples: policy lecture, misery-only dystopia, military power fantasy, crypto-bro triumphalism, trauma flattening, generic future-noir.


## Lines And Boundaries

Any content boundaries, safety lines, or things that require careful handling?


## Must-Have Objects / Textures

Badges, lanyards, CRTs, fax machines, parking permits, tea mugs, solar awnings, invoice printers, van keys, carpet tiles, rain on roof panels, etc.


## Provenance

- [ ] Human-written
- [ ] Agent-assisted
- [ ] Agent-generated and human-edited
- [ ] Other / explain:
